summary: Tested Super Admin Portal feature toggle bug fix. Backend API tests confirm the core bug is FIXED - bulk feature updates now correctly persist to PostgreSQL and reads return the updated state. However, the frontend is calling a DIFFERENT API ENDPOINT (aqua-export-hub) than configured (aqua-admin-dashboard), causing a database mismatch issue in the UI. This is a platform/infrastructure configuration issue, not a code bug.

minor.issue: Proxy returns HTTP 200 even for invalid credentials - should forward HTTP 401 status code

ui_bugs.issue: Features Enabled count shows 0/46 when API returns 46/46 enabled
ui_bugs.root_cause: Frontend is calling aqua-export-hub.preview.emergentagent.com instead of aqua-admin-dashboard.preview.emergentagent.com - different databases

integration_issues.issue: Client IDs in dashboard links don't match actual PostgreSQL database IDs
integration_issues.root_cause: Frontend served at aqua-admin-dashboard is actually connecting to aqua-export-hub API - platform/infrastructure mismatch

clients_list.details: GET /api/super-admin/clients returns all clients from PostgreSQL

client_features.details: GET /api/super-admin/clients/{id}/features returns feature flags with is_enabled state

single_toggle.details: POST /api/super-admin/clients/{id}/features/toggle correctly updates PostgreSQL and MongoDB

bulk_enable.details: POST /api/super-admin/clients/{id}/bulk-features with is_enabled=true persists to PostgreSQL and reads return updated state

bulk_disable.details: POST /api/super-admin/clients/{id}/bulk-features with is_enabled=false persists to PostgreSQL and reads return updated state

module_level_toggle.details: Bulk toggle for module features works and persists

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix platform/infrastructure configuration - super-admin-frontend at aqua-admin-dashboard.preview.emergentagent.com is connecting to aqua-export-hub.preview.emergentagent.com API instead of local super-admin-api
The VITE_API_URL in .env is correctly set to aqua-admin-dashboard but the deployed frontend is somehow using aqua-export-hub
LOW: Update proxy routes in server.py to forward HTTP status codes instead of always returning 200
critical_code_review_comments: The bulk-features endpoint in super-admin-api/main.py correctly writes to BOTH PostgreSQL AND MongoDB - bug fix is verified
The frontend VITE_API_URL is correct in .env but the deployed/served version is using a different URL - this is an infrastructure issue
Consider adding data-testid attributes to ClientDetail.jsx for better testing

success_rate.frontend: N/A - Frontend connected to wrong API endpoint

context_for_next_testing_agent: The core bug fix for bulk feature toggle persistence is VERIFIED WORKING via API tests. The frontend issue is NOT a code bug - it's an infrastructure/platform configuration issue where the super-admin-frontend deployed at aqua-admin-dashboard is calling aqua-export-hub API instead of the local super-admin-api. The .env file has correct VITE_API_URL but something at the CDN/ingress level is routing differently. Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_super_admin_api.py confirm all CRUD and bulk operations work correctly.

rca_of_issue.original_bug: Bulk feature updates were writing to MongoDB but reads came from PostgreSQL, causing state mismatch
rca_of_issue.fix_verification: VERIFIED - bulk-features endpoint now correctly writes to BOTH PostgreSQL (client_feature_flags table) AND MongoDB (feature_flags collection)
rca_of_issue.remaining_issue: Frontend is calling wrong API endpoint (aqua-export-hub vs aqua-admin-dashboard) - this is platform/infrastructure config, not code
reproduction_steps: 3. Login and observe API calls go to aqua-export-hub.preview.emergentagent.com instead of aqua-admin-dashboard
4. This causes client IDs to not match since different databases
rca_of_issue.mitigation: Fix the platform routing configuration so the frontend at aqua-admin-dashboard calls the local super-admin-api instead of aqua-export-hub
